item.title: For the first time ever, the iconic Mister Rogers' Neighborhood will be available to watch on YouTube starting today 6/6/2026.
item.description: An official channel dedicated solely to the beloved program will offer viewers of multiple generations the chance to watch full episodes, livestreams, clips, and compilations all in one place. "With its timeless messages of care and kindness, Mister Rogers' Neighborhood is as relevant now as when it first aired. We're excited to make the series more available for families and fans with this official YouTube destination." - Ellen Doherty, chief creative officer and executive producer, Fred Rogers Productions. Mister Rogers' Neighborhood aired across the United States from 1968 until 2001, with the final episode airing 25 years ago. In total, there were 895 episodes, 200 songs, and 14 characters. https://www.youtube.com/@MisterRogersNeighborhood/videos
item.dc:creator: kenistod

item.title: Keep up the fight, people of Albania!

item.title: Carefully Curated Crap, Vol. 866
item.description:
1

16

18
19
20
21
22
23
24
25
26 Man Tax: Aaron Friesz
"Get all you can, without hurting your soul, your body, or your neighbor. Save all you can, cutting off every needless expense. Give all you can." - John Wesley So, I made a discord server. It's a relaxed environment where you can take/donate memes to the cause, play trivia twice a week, chat about lots of topics or about nothing at all. We also have Jack Box game night and are setting up movie night! Very laid back/relaxed vibe. Come hang with us. https://discord.gg/AWFV7FT3RQ I'm also on the imgcat server for updates & fun: https://discord.gg/TpjD37jgJ9 https://ko-fi.com/mememuseumcurator Unable to work, zero income. Feel free to leave me a tip. Those that have, y'all are the bee's knees. You've honestly been my lifeline. For the couple people that have said I'm "scamming" people, I only get SNAP, so this money goes to things like TP, shampoo, laundry detergent, etc. Tag me if you see an artist/creator you like whose work isn't getting seen as much as it should be! Or if you are an artist/creator and want me to post something of yours, **Imgur Creator Spotlight is now in your hands!! Having trouble finding people I haven't already featured recently. Now I'm going to just do who YOU nominate. Tag me or DM me and I'll feature whoever you pick.**
item.dc:creator: MemeMuseumCurator

item.title: Bach, performed on an accordion.
item.description: This kind of breaks my brain. It's a hell of a lot better than you'd expect. An accordion is actually in the same basic class of instruments as an organ, so it's possible to play organ pieces (with some modification, of course) on an accordion. Full 8:38 video, and it's worth it: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_6aUYKii_Zk
item.dc:creator: PballQhead

item.title: GovBrief Today #492
item.description: Activists are turning 17,000 pounds of the government's own printed Epstein files into a public archive in Washington, with Trump's name on the door. The "Trump and Epstein Memorial Reading Room" opens in days, timed to his birthday next week, and holds 3,400 volumes. Every page the DOJ released is now printed and bound, there to read in your hands instead of on a screen that scrolls it away. The fury over the rape and abuse of children was never a left or right question, and it remains one of the few things this country still agrees on. After stonewalling for years, Trump had no choice but to sign the law that got these files released. Now they sit in the capital under his name and Epstein's. I've emailed 7,716 stories over 492 straight nights, free and without ads. The algorithm can't show them all to you. Add your email address at govbrief.today and I will. #GovBriefToday #Resist Subscribe (It's free!): https://gbounacos.substack.com/ Today's full brief: https://go.govbrief.today/260605-full [1] https://go.govbrief.today/anachronistic-dream [2] https://go.govbrief.today/iran-war-june6 [3] https://go.govbrief.today/hegseth-dday [4] https://go.govbrief.today/krishnan-leaving [5] https://go.govbrief.today/buyer-pardon [6] https://go.govbrief.today/epstein-reading-room Epstein Timeline: https://go.govbrief.today/epstein-crimes Subscribe (It's free!): https://gbounacos.substack.com/
item.dc:creator: GeorgeBounacos

item.description: It’s all fine and dandy killing off his vanity projects, but the only way to actually call a halt to this nonsense is to vote. Check your registration vote.org ! Keep checking it the republicans know that they can’t win on policy so they will do everything to cheat and steal!

item.title: With Republicans facing grim poll figures, Trump promised action to influence the vote citing debunked fraud claims
item.dc:creator: LunaAtlas

item.title: Some questioned how handing out reprints of an editorial published in the ADA’s own journal, at the ADA’s own annual conference, could be construed as a violation of that code.
item.description: https://arstechnica.com/science/2026/06/scientists-ejected-from-diabetes-conference-for-distributing-journal-reprints The article spells the name of the doctor in the picture "Steven Kahn." Ars Technica kindly shared the editorial these five tried to distribute here: https://diabetesjournals.org/care/article/49/6/901/164764/Misguided-Brushes-of-a-Pen-Continue-to-Dismantle I wondered why former ADA president Desmond Schatz of the University of Florida, Gainesville is no longer ADA president. Was he sacked? If so, there might be more to this story, but ADA presidents are elected for one year, and they don't run for reelection.
item.dc:creator: MenloPart
